text: When we first kind of got a demo of console, our Slack internal channel was kind of blowing
up with excitement and getting all like the potential of this is amazing.
I'm Sam Williams, head of IT here at Synthesia.
I'm responsible for the tech stack that employees use.
Obviously console has some amazing features, but if I had to pick one, it really would
be the assistant within console.
Being able to prompt it within a request is great, but it also understands the request
and suggests and tells you what it thinks it should do.
The accuracy of that is actually very impressive.
It is literally like they're an IT employee here at Synthesia.
We've gone from replying within the request to just talking to the assistant and letting
the assistant do the work.
I would now probably count in a funny way the console assistant as an employee in my
team.
The bit that really got us going was the ability for us to write the automation, or as console
calls it, playbooks in pure natural language was a game changer for us.
For general support, the average resolve time, I think off the top of my head, the JSM was
easy four or five hours just for first time responses.
Console replies in a matter of minutes, not even that.
I think it's under a minute right now.
The automation is saving us easily 20x.
We were going from about 25% automated in JSM.
We're now sitting about 70 to 75% automation and seeing that kind of figure rise every
week or even every day.
My core responsibility is that the tech stack that we use at Synthesia doesn't just work
for IT.
It has to work for multiple teams, but also for the employees.
Kind of biggest concern that console put to rest was the fact that it worked for not
just IT, but it worked for multiple teams.
Their focus wasn't just solving IT's problem, it was solving every operational team's problem.
To be able to see it handle not only IT requests, but the people team requests, DevOps requests,
integrations with our HR system, which is Hi Bob.
That integrations with Salesforce, knowing that that was already there in place, was
imperative for us to get other teams by and for console.
That was the moment I was like, I made the right decision here because I just managed
to support 600 odd employees on my own.
We've become very reliant on it.
If console disappears for us, I think it would be detrimental to my team and also for the
business.
I remember putting in a request about a feature and then I woke up the next day and the feature
was live and I was like, okay, this is impressive, but maybe it's just a one off and it just
happened day after day, week after week.
It's set a very high bar for other vendors that we work with.
Console team has set that standard for us.
